response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web engineer whose core expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and modern web/cloud tooling — none of which aligns with the mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years required on Windows platforms. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, no Windows-platform development background, and no meaningful capital markets or fixed income domain experience. His stint at RIDE Capital involved investment-adjacent context but used Node/GraphQL, not C++ or financial systems infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated C/C++ exposure at National Instruments building measurement components for a commercial engineering IDE
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via public talks, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad polyglot engineering background with exposure to low-latency systems (Sociomantic) and financial-services context (RIDE Capital)
concerns: C++ professional experience is approximately 6 months versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows platform
No SQL Server or Oracle experience evident; SQL skills appear limited to MySQL/MongoDB in web contexts
Zero capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking systems knowledge

key_factors.factor: No Windows platform or enterprise DB experience
